route.name: Owens Valley Ramble
route.description: Public GPX Eastern Sierra/Owens Valley overnighter with desert-valley exposure, dirt roads, pavement, and mountain views.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Eastern Sierra/Owens Valley overnighter with desert-valley exposure, dirt roads, pavement, and mountain views.
route.bestSeason: Cooler shoulder seasons are safer; July needs strict heat and water screening.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Eastern Sierra/Owens Valley overnighter with desert-valley exposure, dirt roads, pavement, and mountain views.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water sources and valley heat exposure must be checked carefully for July.
resources.food: Eastern Sierra towns provide before/after services; on-route options require current confirmation.
resources.camping: Camp/lodging or legal dispersed options must be selected and verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Potential California candidate only with a mild forecast.
Measure water gaps from the GPX before promotion.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Owens Valley Ramble still lacks a July heat/water/camp plan; do not promote until those are explicit.
Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Lone Pine Campground is a developed alternate, not proof that the original dispersed-style route camp is solved.
Owens Valley Ramble still needs a chosen night-by-night itinerary and heat/water/fire checks before gate confirmation review.
Must stay rework: no completed source-backed GPX/legal-access overlay for Inyo NF, BLM Alabama Hills/Bishop Field Office, Inyo County/local roads, and any LADWP/day-use-only constraints.
Lone Pine Campground availability is currently real, but campsite availability is not a reservation and does not align the public GPX to the developed-camp anchor.
July 10-13 heat forecast cannot be cleared on July 2 because the full window is beyond reliable 7-day go/no-go forecast range.
Water plan remains source-limited: route guide gives refill concepts, but current potable availability and exact longest dry carry are not verified.
Stage 1 fire restrictions require a jurisdiction-specific stove/camp plan; campfires/charcoal are not gate-confirmed defaults.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Check Bishop/Owens Valley forecast and smoke for July 10-13 and set a heat cutoff.
Measure the longest water gap from the GPX and verify current water points.
Choose a specific legal overnight and confirm rules/availability.
Complete July 9-10 final refresh for next-weekend gate confirmation.
Decide whether Lone Pine Campground is the intended overnight anchor, then reserve the specific site and align mileage and water plan to that anchor.
Book a specific Lone Pine Campground site, preferably site 024 if still available, or record reservation confirmation before promotion.
Create the anchored GPX variant including the Whitney Portal Road campground spur and recompute mileage, gain, exposure, and water gaps.

Overlay the final GPX against Inyo NF MVUM/current alerts, BLM Alabama Hills designated route/camping maps and Bishop Field Office restrictions, Inyo County road status, and any LADWP/day-use-only land constraints.
Verify Manzanar Visitor Center hours/water and Lone Pine/Independence refill points during July 9-10 final refresh.
Set explicit heat/smoke/wind go/no-go thresholds and run a final NWS/air-quality refresh on July 9 or July 10, 2026.
Document fire-compliant cooking: no campfires/charcoal; pressurized gas/LPG stove with shutoff valve only where allowed, with California Campfire Permit if required.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Two-day route shape fits a weekend, but July Owens Valley heat and water exposure need a forecast-based go/no-go.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Existing public GPX is present locally and matches the Bikepacking.com 64-mile, 2-day route description.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Recreation.gov facility 232069, Lone Pine Campground site 024 in Pine loop, Standard Nonelectric, showed Available on July 10, 11, 12, and 13, 2026; group tent site 43 also showed Available. This is not a held booking and does not align the public GPX to that developed-camp anchor.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Inyo NF Stage 1 restrictions and BLM fire restrictions are manageable only with a jurisdiction-specific stove/camp plan; July 10-13 heat/smoke forecast cannot be cleared on July 2.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: No completed GPX-by-segment overlay against Inyo NF MVUM/current closures, BLM Alabama Hills/designated route/camping maps, county road status, and any LADWP/day-use-only constraints.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Route source identifies Independence, Manzanar Visitor Center, Lone Pine, and west-side natural sources, but current operation/potability and the longest dry carry for the anchored GPX are not verified.
promotionChecklist.mapArtifactsNote: After `python3 -m bikepacking_planner build-trip route-library`, exported GPX and GeoJSON exist and route is present in manifest.

days.name: Owens Valley Ramble segment 1
days.camp: Camp/lodging or legal dispersed options must be selected and verified.

waypoints.name: Beware Of Sand As You Descend To Manzanar!

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Inyo National Forest source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/inyo
Inyo Stage 1 Fire Restrictions checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/inyo/alerts/stage-1-fire-restrictions-effect
Camp availability worker found Lone Pine Campground site 024 publicly available for July 10-13, 2026, subject to booking.
Potential rework: Lone Pine Campground anchored version using Recreation.gov facility 232069, but this needs a new anchored GPX and water/heat/legal verification before it can be gate-confirmed.

readinessEvidence.finding: Inyo reports Moderate fire danger for Owens and active Stage 1 Fire Restrictions on Inyo National Forest lands.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Inyo National Forest home and alerts pages

readinessEvidence.finding: Stage 1 Fire Restrictions are effective June 22 through December 31, 2026; route camps/stoves must comply.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Inyo National Forest Stage 1 Fire Restrictions alert

readinessEvidence.finding: Public route page lists a 64-mile, 2-day GPX-backed route; source flags summer heat, east-side camping water carry, and limited water after Lone Pine/Owens River.
Inyo lists Stage 1 Fire Restrictions effective June 22, 2026 and Moderate fire danger for Owens; this does not verify a camp/water plan.
Exported GPX exists after rebuild with 2111 GPX points; GeoJSON and manifest entry also exist.

Recreation.gov public availability for Lone Pine Campground showed site 024, Pine loop, standard nonelectric, available on July 10, 11, 12, and 13, 2026; group tent site 43 was also available across all four dates.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Recreation.gov Lone Pine Campground availability API

readinessEvidence.finding: Lone Pine Campground site 024 and group tent site 43 showed Available on July 10, 11, 12, and 13, 2026 in Recreation.gov public availability, but this is not a reservation and does not align the route to the campground anchor.

Inyo National Forest Stage 1 restrictions are active from 2026-06-22 through 2026-12-31. Campfires/stove fires are prohibited on Inyo NF except listed developed recreation sites; valid CA Campfire Permit holders may use pressurized gas/LPG stoves with shutoff valve.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Inyo National Forest Stage 1 Fire Restrictions

readinessEvidence.finding: Usable route geometry source is identified for planning and local map/export QA.

readinessEvidence.sourceName: Bureau of Land Management Bishop Field Office
readinessEvidence.finding: Official land-manager or route-corridor source identified for route-mile legal access overlay.
